sections.paragraph:
f. ( Gen 13:10 Exo 29:23 2Ki 5:5), cstr. כִּכַּר pr. a circle, globe, for כִּרְכָּר from the root כָּרַר Pi. כִּרְכֵּר (To this there agree in the western languages, circus, circulus, and the letter r being softened, κύκλος; comp. כָּרַךְ.) Specially-

(1) a circumjacent tract of country, der Umkreis, Neh 12:28 כִּכַּר חַיַּרְדֵּן the tract of Jordan, i.e. the region through which the Jordan flows down into the Red Sea; κατʼ ἐξοχήν הַכִּכָּר Gen 13:12 19:17 , sq.; 2Sa 18:23 Gr. ἡ περίχωρος τοῦ Ἰορδάνου, Mat 3:5 now called الفور el Ghôr.

(2) כִּכַּר לֶחֶם a cake, a round loaf, Exo 29:23 1Sa 2:36 Pro 6:26. Pl. m. כִּכְּרֹות לֶחֶם Jud. 8:5 1Sa 10:3.

(3) a talent (Syr. ܟܰܟܪܳܐ), equal, as nearly as can be computed from Exo 38:25, 26 Exo 38:26, to three thousand shekels of the sanctuary, Zec 5:7, כִּכַּר זָהָב a talent of gold; 1Ki 9:14 10:10 , 14 1Ki 10:14. Dual כִּכָּרַיִם two talents, 2Ki 5:23 כִּכְּרַיִם כֶּסֶף two talents of silver, ibid.; where כִּכְּרַיִם holds as it were a middle place between the absolute state כִּכָּרַיִם and the const. כִּכְּרֵי, which could not be used without taking away the numeral distinction. Pl. כִּכָּרִים const. כִּכְּרֵי f. talents, 2Ki 5:5 1Ch 22:14 29:7 Ezr 8:26.
